docs.title: TLS pinning and signed requests (high-threat mobile / web)
docs.text: Tarka’s HTTP SDKs use system TLS (URLSession / OkHttp) by default. For regulated or high-fraud environments, add certificate pinning and optionally signed request bodies so MitM proxies cannot silently rewrite JSON.

docs.title: Android (OkHttp CertificatePinner)
docs.text:
1. Obtain SPKI hashes of your API leaf/intermediate certs (pin backup pins for rotation).
  2. Build a CertificatePinner and attach to the OkHttpClient used by your fork of DecisionClient (or subclass).
val pinner = CertificatePinner.Builder()
    .add("api.example.com", "sha256/AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=")
    .build()
val client = OkHttpClient.Builder()
    .certificatePinner(pinner)
    .build()
  1. Set metadata.tls_pinning_verified: true on evaluate requests only after a successful pin-verified connection (Decision API reads this for integrity hints — see integrity_policy.py).

docs.title: iOS (URLSession delegate)
docs.text: Use URLSessionDelegate with urlSession(_:didReceive:completionHandler:) and SecTrust + pinned SPKI or public key. Wire the session into a custom DecisionClient if you fork the SDK.

docs.title: Web
docs.text: Prefer HTTPS + CORS-correct API; pinning in browsers is limited. Use Subresource Integrity for static assets; for API calls, rely on SameSite cookies + short-lived tokens and server-side attestation.

docs.title: Optional signed request helper
docs.text:
Pattern: X-Tarka-Timestamp + X-Tarka-Signature = HMAC-SHA256 over timestamp + "\n" + raw_body with a shared secret rotated per tenant.

Canonical helpers: services/shared/tarka_request_signature.py and packages/fraud-sdk-python (fraud_stack_sdk.request_signing).

  • Server (decision-api): set REQUEST_SIGNATURE_SECRET to enable optional verification on POST /v1/decisions/evaluate (same HMAC). When unset, evaluate is unchanged (many teams still terminate signing at Envoy / Kong / Cloudflare).
  • Client: compute HMAC in the app and add headers; never embed the secret in the client for public apps — use per-install keys from your backend or mTLS instead.

Edge gateways with mTLS to decision-api remain a common pattern for multi-hop setups.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• services/decision-api — metadata.tls_pinning_verified in evaluate path
  • SDK scorecard
  • Regulated markets feature pack — optional checklist for fintech / banking / crypto-style deployments

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Restore analyst trust and workflow coherence across Cases, Case Detail, Investigation, and Rules by fixing reliability blockers first, then tightening grounding/evidence guarantees, then simplifying the end-to-end analyst journey.

docs.title: Guiding Principles
docs.text:
1. Trust before throughput: if a control appears available, it must be reliable and truthful.
  2. Fail transparently: all degraded states must be explicit, actionable, and scoped.
  3. Case-centric coherence: queue -> case -> investigate -> decide should feel like one workflow.
  4. Evidence over appearance: grounding, claim provenance, and freshness indicators are first-class.

docs.title: Priority Structure
docs.text:
• P0 (stability blockers): unblock core case and rules workflows.
  • P1 (trust hardening): prevent misleading partial functionality.
  • P2 (coherence & adoption): reduce cognitive load and align language.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
• Stop user harm from misleading states.
  • Prevent analysts from making decisions off broken context.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
1. Feature gate broken surfaces
  2. Disable Rules navigation target when rules page crash condition is detected.
  3. Disable case-dependent Investigation quick actions when case context APIs fail.
  4. Add temporary degraded banners
  5. Global status card in Cases and Case Detail showing service outage context.
  6. Error message uplift (hotfix)
  7. Replace raw 500 Internal Server Error with structured fallback copy:
    • What failed
    • What still works
    • What to do next
  8. Ops escalation
  9. Create incident channel and owner rotation for ongoing failures.

docs.title: Exit Criteria
docs.text:
• No silent partial workflows.
  • Analysts can distinguish healthy vs degraded paths instantly.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text: • Make core analyst flows executable end-to-end.

docs.title: Workstream A: Case Workflow Reliability
docs.text:
1. Fix list endpoint chain:
  2. /v1/cases
  3. /v1/case-views
  4. /v1/cases/playbooks
  5. Fix case detail retrieval path (/v1/cases/{id}).
  6. Fix create-case path (POST /v1/cases) including idempotency guard.
  7. Add API contract tests for:
  8. list
  9. detail
  10. create
  11. views
  12. playbooks

docs.title: Workstream B: Rules Availability
docs.text:
1. Null-safe normalize packs in frontend.
  2. Filter out non-rule metadata packs from rule-card rendering.
  3. Add defensive error boundary for Rules route.
  4. Add fixture coverage for mixed pack schemas.

docs.title: Exit Criteria
docs.text:
• Cases list load success >= 99%.
  • Case detail load success >= 99%.
  • Create case success >= 98% (excluding validation errors).
  • Rules crash-free sessions >= 99.9%.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text: • Ensure analysts can trust what is grounded, verified, and fresh.

docs.title: Workstream C: Grounding Integrity
docs.text:
1. Introduce explicit Investigation grounding status:
  2. Grounded
  3. Partially grounded
  4. Ungrounded
  5. Block or annotate actions requiring unavailable context.
  6. Add one-click retry for failed context fetches.

docs.title: Workstream D: Evidence & Claim Contract
docs.text:
1. Enforce structured claim trailer generation in copilot responses.
  2. Fail closed for high-risk responses missing provenance metadata.
  3. Add telemetry:
  4. claim-contract compliance rate
  5. grounded-response rate
  6. unsupported-claim rate

docs.title: Workstream E: Trust Signaling
docs.text:
1. Make sidebar counts health-aware (degraded marker + freshness timestamp).
  2. Add stale data markers to queue metrics and badges.

docs.title: Exit Criteria
docs.text:
• Grounded-response rate visible in dashboard and stable.
  • Unknown/unverified claim warnings reduced by target threshold.
  • No confidence-signaling badges during backend outage.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text: • Reduce onboarding friction and unify analyst journey language.

docs.title: Workstream F: UX Coherence
docs.text:
1. Replace endpoint-centric UI strings with analyst-intent copy.
  2. Progressive disclosure for preset catalog:
  3. top starter presets
  4. advanced grouped sections
  5. Case-centric flow polish:
  6. clearer transitions between queue, detail, and copilot
  7. persistent case context identity chips and health state

docs.title: Workstream G: Unified Error Experience
docs.text:
1. Standard error contract schema across services:
  2. code
  3. message
  4. retryable
  5. support_id
  6. Shared frontend error renderer with remediation guidance.

docs.title: Exit Criteria
docs.text:
• First-task completion time reduced for new analysts.
  • Reduced support escalations tied to generic 500 errors.
  • Positive qualitative feedback on workflow clarity.

docs.title: Ownership Model
docs.text:
• Engineering Manager (Risk Ops Platform): overall delivery and risk tradeoffs.
  • Frontend Lead: workflow coherence, degraded-state UX, error renderer.
  • Case API Lead: list/detail/create reliability and contract stability.
  • Decision/Rules Lead: rules payload normalization and route stability.
  • Investigation Lead: grounding integrity and claim provenance enforcement.
  • SRE/Platform: alerting, observability, incident readiness.
  • Risk Ops Design/PM: copy and task-flow validation with analysts.

docs.title: Verification and Reporting Cadence
docs.text:
• Daily standup: blocker and incident review.
  • Twice-weekly reliability scorecard:
  • list/detail/create success
  • rules crash-free rate
  • investigation grounded-response rate
  • Weekly stakeholder review:
  • trust risk burn-down
  • workflow coherence demos

docs.title: Backlog Reference
docs.text:
Importable Jira backlog CSV:

  • docs/docs/guides/jira-backlog-trust-workflow.csv

Recommended order: execute all Highest priority issues first, then High, then Medium.

docs.title: Unit21 Fraud DAO / Fraud Consortium vs Tarka consortium_api (pattern comparison)
docs.text:
Disclaimer: This note is not a partnership, certification, or recommendation of any vendor. Unit21 is named only because their public materials describe a widely cited shared fraud intelligence model. Product names, APIs, hashing details, and membership rules change over time; verify against Unit21’s current product pages and your counsel before any integration.

Naming: In Unit21’s marketing, “Fraud DAO” and “Fraud Consortium” refer to the same network product (they also use fraud-dao.com as a consumer-facing entry). This is not on-chain DAO governance in the smart-contract sense; it is consortium-style collaboration on de-identified fraud signals.

docs.title: Unit21 (public marketing pattern)
docs.text:
From Unit21’s blogs and product copy, the consortium emphasizes:

  1. Screening — Check users at onboarding and at payment initiation against shared network intelligence.
  2. Ongoing monitoring — Lifecycle monitoring of existing customers, not only first touch.
  3. Alerts — Real-time, rules-engine-driven alerts with contextual logic inside the Unit21 platform (not described as a standalone OSS rules surface).
  4. Share / label — One-click (or streamlined) contribution of confirmed fraud, with standardized “Blocked Reasons” (e.g. account takeover, scams, stolen identity categories in their examples) so the network updates.

They describe privacy-preserving sharing using proprietary / patent-pending hashing so PII is not exposed in the raw form members would recognize across firms. Scale and membership figures in their materials are marketing claims; treat operational SLAs and legal bases as contract-specific. They also describe folding the network into the broader Unit21 platform (screening, rules, cases); details evolve with their product roadmap.

docs.title: Public references (verify current)
docs.text:
• Fraud Consortium product
  • fraud-dao.com (marketing entry point; may redirect or mirror product messaging)
  • Fraud DAO expansion / features blog (historical feature narrative; not a spec)

docs.title: Tarka consortium_api (decision-api)
docs.text:
Tarka implements a self-hosted, Redis-backed aggregate keyed by hash_entity_id(consortium_secret, tenant_id, entity_id) under fraud:consortium:*, gated by CONSORTIUM_ENABLED.

Step HTTP (prefix /v1/consortium) Role Share signal POST /share signal_type, severity, reporter tenant_id; merges multi-tenant reports and trust weights. Lookup GET /check/{tenant_id}/{entity_id} Read aggregate for that hash (reporter tenant list stripped from response). Feedback POST /feedback false_positive | confirmed_fraud — drives false_positive_rate and scoring penalty. Trust POST /trust Per-tenant trust_score (0.1–2.0) for weighted contribution.

Evaluate path: POST /v1/evaluate calls check_consortium_signal and applies consortium_score_delta() (minimum distinct reporters, caps, FP penalty). Implementation: consortium_api.py, redis_store.py, consortium.py, main.py under services/decision-api/.

Tarka does not ship a labeled “Blocked Reasons” taxonomy matching Unit21’s; you would map your own reason codes to signal_type strings and/or rules. There is no built-in Unit21 API client or entity UID graph from their network.

docs.title: Conceptual mapping
docs.text: Idea Unit21 (as publicly described) Tarka Screen / query Network lookup at onboarding or pay-in GET /v1/consortium/check/... and/or implicit check on /v1/evaluate Shared badness signal De-identified consortium hit + platform rules Aggregated signal_type counts + max_severity + trust-weighted quality Confirm / share back One-click fraud label → network update POST /v1/consortium/share (new/stronger signal) and/or POST /v1/consortium/feedback Standardized labels “Blocked Reasons” categories Your taxonomy → signal_type (string) conventions Rules + workflow Unit21 rules engine + case tooling Your rules (YAML/OPA/etc.) + Case API / investigation flows

docs.title: Integration sketch (adapter only)
docs.text:
For calling Tarka’s consortium endpoints directly (share / check / feedback / trust / batch ingest), use scripts/consortium_adapter/README.md.

If you use both a Unit21-backed network and Tarka:

  1. Inbound: Map Unit21 screening or alert payloads (scores, flags, categories) into /v1/evaluate features/tags or a thin service that writes /v1/consortium/share with a stable entity_id and agreed signal_type vocabulary — only where contracts and privacy reviews allow.
  2. Outbound: Map confirmed fraud / false positive outcomes from your ops queue into /v1/consortium/feedback (and optionally /share) so local consortium math matches your governance.

Do not assume hash compatibility between Unit21’s network and Tarka’s consortium_secret hash; a bridge almost always treats the vendor as opaque and maps to your entity key + your consortium lane (CONSORTIUM_ID).

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Same pattern class, different public operator: joinsonar-query-feedback-vs-consortium-api.md.
  • Release context: releases/v1.2.0-2026-05-30.md.
  • Vendor-neutral framing: competitive-critical-review-2026-04.md.

docs.title: v1.2.5 execution backlog — resiliency, ETL stability, no-code rules
docs.text:
Branch train: ide/v1.2.5-7320 (merge epics via focused PRs; link each PR to this doc section).

Ordering (fastest ROI first): ETL contract + quality gates → no-code rule workflow → degradation/SLO burn → rule telemetry → chaos + replay certification.

docs.title: Epic R1 — Platform hardening & SLOs
docs.text: ID Deliverable Surfaces CI / ops R1.1 SLO definitions (availability, p95/p99 latency, queue lag) per service: decision-api, case-api, integration-ingress, feature-service, ml-scoring docs/docs/guides/service-slos-v1.md (new), link from deployment Optional: Prometheus recording rules template in deploy/observability/ R1.2 /v1/slo parity — extend each service with targets + current counters (where missing) FastAPI handlers Contract tests R1.3 Burn-rate alerts — fast window (5m) + slow window (1h) doc + example Alertmanager / Grafana deploy/observability/ README Nightly validate YAML R1.4 Error rate by route — middleware label path + tenant_id where safe Done — http_requests_total + http_client_errors_total / http_server_errors_total with tenant_query = present or absent (query param only); see service-slos-v1.md § HTTP metrics Dashboard JSON optional

docs.title: Epic R2 — Failure isolation & degradation
docs.text: ID Deliverable API / code R2.1 Circuit breaker wrapper for httpx outbound (graph, ML, feature, OPA, currency) services/shared/ or per-service circuit.py R2.2 Tags on degrade — enrichment:unavailable, ml:unavailable, graph:unavailable; inference driver + tier bump decision-api inference_build / evaluate path R2.3 Per-tenant flags — Redis or DB keyed tenant:{id}:flags (kill-switch enrichers) decision-api + small admin GET R2.4 ML down → rules-only — already partial; unify reason string + audit field fallback_reason evaluate + OpenAPI

docs.title: Epic R3 — Idempotency & exactly-once-ish
docs.text: ID Deliverable Status R3.1 Require Idempotency-Key on high-volume ingest paths (config flag) event-ingest INGEST_REQUIRE_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY R3.2 Decision evaluate — document + optional require header (gateway) Done — TARKA_EVALUATE_REQUIRE_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY; 422 + evaluate_idempotency_required when enabled and header missing; GET /v1/slo current.evaluate_require_idempotency_key; api-reference.md R3.3 ETL dedupe — bronze table unique key (source, event_id) + quarantine table future: analytics-sink / warehouse R3.4 Consumer idempotency — decision worker uses trace key from payload event-ingest consumer

docs.title: Epic R4 — Chaos & recovery
docs.text: ID Deliverable R4.1 scripts/chaos/README.md + compose override profiles (stop redis, delay postgres) R4.2 GitHub workflow chaos-smoke (optional manual dispatch) running health + evaluate after fault inject R4.3 Runbook template in docs/docs/guides/runbook-chaos-template.md

docs.title: Epic E1 — Contract-first ingestion (first slice shipped)
docs.text:
ID Deliverable Location E1.1 Ingest envelope v1 — optional wrapper { "schema_version": "1", "event": { ... } } + INGEST_ENVELOPE_MODE optional|required services/event-ingest E1.2 Reject/quarantine — 422 + reason_codes[] for unknown event_type, empty ids, wrong schema same E1.3 Metrics — ingest_contract_reject_total, envelope version counter Prometheus text E1.4 Compatibility policy doc — backward compatible until schema_version bump this file § below

Compatibility: schema_version omitted = legacy flat body (unchanged). schema_version: "1" requires event object. Breaking changes only bump schema major.

docs.title: Epic E2 — Bronze / Silver / Gold
docs.text: ID Deliverable E2.1 Doc: physical mapping (NATS raw → worker → Postgres audit = bronze-ish; gold = aggregates) — etl-bronze-silver-gold.md E2.2 scripts/etl/check_silver_features.py — JSONL null/enum/amount checks + max violation rate E2.3 INGEST_DLQ_* + scripts/etl/replay_dlq.py — DLQ publish on evaluate 4xx (opt-in); replay CLI

docs.title: Epic E3 — Late arrival & watermark (shipped)

docs.text: ID Deliverable UI N1.1 Simulation API — POST batch of features → shadow decision delta histogram decision-api or new rules-sim N1.2 Rule composer — extend static/rule-builder.html or React wizard frontend N1.3 Impact summary — top tags / entities from sim (sampled) API response

docs.title: Epic X — Cross-cutting metadata
docs.text: ID Deliverable Status X.1 counter_version, rule_pack_file, ml_model on every audit row (partially exists — unify) Done — payload_snapshot + GET /v1/audit/{trace_id}; counter_version from AGG_KEY_VERSION (default default); rule_pack_file = comma-sorted contributing JSON pack filenames; ml_model duplicated from inference_context X.2 etl_batch_id optional lineage (ingest → evaluate audit) Done (v1) — optional etl_batch_id on v1 envelope root merged into event.metadata; decision-api payload_snapshot + GET /v1/audit expose etl_batch_id when present in metadata X.3 Replay parity gate in CI — threshold in scripts/replay/ README Done — scripts/replay/README.md § CI parity gate; .github/workflows/counter-parity-smoke.yml X.4 Tenant reliability profile — strict|balanced|permissive env doc + decision-api read Done — TARKA_TENANT_RELIABILITY_PROFILE; GET /v1/ops/evaluation-posture → tenant_reliability_profile; deployment-profiles-community-vs-pro.md

docs.title: Release gates (v1.2.5)
docs.text:
Block tag / branch protection checks when:

  1. SLO burn synthetic test fails (once R1.3 exists).
  2. ETL contract reject rate integration test fails (once E4.2 + CI wired).
  3. Replay parity below threshold (existing scripts + new gate).

docs.title: Ready-to-run examples
docs.text:
These guides are copy-paste oriented: they assume you can start a stack from sandbox-five-minute.md or full deployment.md profiles, then follow curls and UI paths.

Example Focus Stack Credit-card fraud (rules + ONNX) Decision API + ml-scoring + rules Lite or core + ml API bot / credential-stuffing defense Device-style payloads, velocity, ingress hardening Lite IOC enrichment + graph (cyber) OSINT aggregation + Neo4j subgraph Full or graph + integration

For synthetic benchmarks (latency / throughput on your hardware), see scripts/benchmarks/README.md.

For simulation-based A/B and vertical packs (no live traffic), see shadow-and-ab-testing.md.

docs.title: Example: API bot / credential-stuffing defense
docs.text: Goal: Exercise device and behavior-style signals, velocity in inference_context, and awareness of ingress replay-style hardening (Redis-backed duplicate detection on the Decision path when configured).

docs.title: 1. Stack
docs.text:
Use lite compose (Decision + Case + Ingress + UI):

docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.lite.yml up -d --build

docs.title: 2. Bot-like session (no human-like behavior)
docs.text:
Tight velocity + automation hints:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/decisions/evaluate \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "tenant_id": "demo",
    "event_type": "login",
    "entity_id": "acct-brute-9001",
    "payload": {
      "event_count_5m": 40,
      "event_count_1h": 200,
      "event_count_24h": 800,
      "is_bot": true,
      "is_emulator": true,
      "webdriver_detected": true,
      "headless_detected": true,
      "hour_of_day": 2
    },
    "device_context": {
      "device_id": "auto-headless",
      "platform": "web",
      "signals": {
        "is_emulator": true,
        "is_vpn": false,
        "is_spoofed_location": false,
        "is_bot": true,
        "is_repackaged": false,
        "webdriver_detected": true,
        "headless_detected": true,
        "automation_detected": true,
        "vpn_interface_detected": false,
        "mock_location_detected": false,
        "timezone_geo_mismatch": false,
        "canvas_fp_hash": null,
        "webgl_renderer": "Google SwiftShader",
        "screen_res": "800x600",
        "touch_support": false,
        "battery_api_present": false,
        "language": "en-US",
        "platform_version": "HeadlessChrome",
        "captcha": null,
        "audio_fp_hash": null,
        "connection_type": "4g",
        "device_memory": 4,
        "hardware_concurrency": 2,
        "color_depth": 24,
        "timezone": "Etc/UTC",
        "timezone_offset": 0,
        "do_not_track": "1",
        "cookie_enabled": false,
        "local_storage_available": false,
        "session_storage_available": false,
        "indexed_db_available": false,
        "max_touch_points": 0,
        "pdf_viewer_enabled": null
      },
      "attestation": null,
      "behavior": null
    }
  }'

Expect elevated replay_risk / tamper-related drivers when ingress and payload patterns align with configured rules; recommended_action may suggest step-up or block.

docs.title: 3. Credential stuffing pattern
docs.text: Repeat the same request with different entity_id but identical high-velocity payload shape to simulate many accounts per IP (in production you would vary metadata / IP-derived fields if your rules use them).

docs.title: 4. Client SDK
docs.text: The TypeScript package @tarka/sdk can collect device signals in-browser for real integrations (see packages/fraud-sdk-typescript). This example uses explicit JSON for clarity.

docs.title: 5. Ingress
docs.text:
For OSINT on abusive IPs (parallel enrichment), use Integration Ingress (port 8003 in lite):

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8003/v1/osint \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ip":"198.51.100.10","email":null,"phone":null,"domain":null}'

See adding-osint-source.md to add providers.

docs.title: Example: Classic payment fraud — rules + ONNX autoencoder
docs.text: Goal: Show a high-risk payment evaluation with inference_context, rule hits, and optional ML scoring via ml-scoring (heuristic by default; ONNX when configured).

docs.title: 1. Start services
docs.text:
Option A — Lite (heuristic ML only, no separate ml-scoring container):

docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.lite.yml up -d --build

Point Decision API at ML service only if you run ml-scoring separately (see Option B).

Option B — Core + ML (from repo root, under deploy/):

docker compose -f docker-compose.yml --profile core --profile ml up -d --build

Set ML_SCORING_URL=http://ml-scoring:8005 on decision-api (already wired in full docker-compose.yml when profiles include ml).

docs.title: 2. ONNX (optional)
docs.text:
See ONNX model integration. Quick path:

  1. Build ml-scoring with optional onnx extra or install onnxruntime in the image.
  2. Mount a model and set ONNX_MODEL_PATH (and DISABLE_ML=false).

Without ONNX, ml-scoring still returns heuristic + explainability signals usable by the Decision API.

docs.title: 3. Evaluate a “card-not-present” style event
docs.text:
High amount + velocity + risky device flags:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/decisions/evaluate \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "tenant_id": "demo",
    "event_type": "payment",
    "entity_id": "card-user-001",
    "payload": {
      "amount": 9200,
      "currency": "USD",
      "merchant_mcc": "5411",
      "event_count_5m": 8,
      "event_count_1h": 35,
      "event_count_24h": 120,
      "is_new_device": true,
      "is_vpn": true,
      "hour_of_day": 3
    },
    "device_context": {
      "device_id": "web-abc",
      "platform": "web",
      "signals": {
        "is_emulator": false,
        "is_vpn": true,
        "is_spoofed_location": false,
        "is_bot": false,
        "is_repackaged": false,
        "webdriver_detected": false,
        "headless_detected": false,
        "automation_detected": false,
        "vpn_interface_detected": true,
        "mock_location_detected": false,
        "timezone_geo_mismatch": true,
        "canvas_fp_hash": null,
        "webgl_renderer": null,
        "screen_res": "1920x1080",
        "touch_support": false,
        "battery_api_present": true,
        "language": "en-US",
        "platform_version": null,
        "captcha": null,
        "audio_fp_hash": null,
        "connection_type": null,
        "device_memory": 8,
        "hardware_concurrency": 8,
        "color_depth": 24,
        "timezone": "UTC",
        "timezone_offset": 0,
        "do_not_track": null,
        "cookie_enabled": true,
        "local_storage_available": true,
        "session_storage_available": true,
        "indexed_db_available": true,
        "max_touch_points": 0,
        "pdf_viewer_enabled": null
      },
      "attestation": null,
      "behavior": null
    }
  }'

Inspect inference_context (tier, driver_reasons, velocity), recommended_action, rule_hits, and ml_score if ML URL is configured.

docs.title: 4. Rules
docs.text: JSON rules live under services/decision-api/rules/. Adjust thresholds via env on Decision API (DENY_THRESHOLD, REVIEW_THRESHOLD) per deployment.md.

docs.title: 5. Accuracy / datasets
docs.text: For reproducible precision/recall on synthetic labeled data, use POST /v1/simulation/run and POST /v1/simulation/benchmark/vertical (see shadow-and-ab-testing.md). Public Kaggle-style benchmarks are not bundled; export features in your notebook and POST to /v1/decisions/evaluate in batch if you need offline scoring.

docs.title: Example: IOC enrichment + threat-actor style clustering (graph)
docs.text: Goal: Combine parallel OSINT (IP / domain context) with a live Neo4j-backed subgraph for entity linkage. This matches a cyber use case (IOC pivoting); fraud teams can use the same pattern for mule rings or shared devices.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Neo4j + graph-service + integration-ingress + decision-api (and optionally case-api / UI).

From repo root:

cd deploy
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml --profile core --profile graph --profile integration --profile cases --profile ui up -d --build

Ports: Decision 8000, Graph 8001, Case 8002, Ingress 8003, Frontend 3000, Neo4j browser 7474.

docs.title: 1. OSINT on an IOC
docs.text:
IP (Shodan / GreyNoise / AbuseIPDB / etc. — keys optional, see .env.example):

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8003/v1/osint \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ip":"8.8.8.8","email":null,"phone":null,"domain":"example.com"}'

List configured sources:

curl -s http://localhost:8003/v1/osint/sources

docs.title: 2. Upsert graph entities (via graph-service API)
docs.text: Use your deployment’s graph upsert endpoints (see services/graph-service OpenAPI or contracts/openapi) to create User / IP / Domain nodes and RELATED_TO edges. The Graph Explorer in the UI (/graph) visualizes neighborhoods when the backend is reachable.

docs.title: 3. Decision + graph context
docs.text:
Evaluate an event tied to the same entity_id you stored in Neo4j so Decision API can include graph-derived features when GRAPH_SERVICE_URL is set:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/decisions/evaluate \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "tenant_id": "demo",
    "event_type": "custom",
    "entity_id": "ioc-entity-1",
    "payload": {"ioc_type": "ip", "value": "198.51.100.55", "severity_hint": "high"}
  }'

Inspect inference_context.top_signals and graph-related fields when the graph integration path is active.

docs.title: 4. MITRE-style tagging (roadmap)
docs.text: Fine-grained MITRE ATT&CK labels are not fully wired as first-class fields in v1.1; track borrowed-from-OSS / typology issues for structured tactic/technique mapping. Until then, use tags on evaluate responses and case labels for analyst workflow.

docs.title: 5. Lite stack without Neo4j
docs.text: Use mock graph behavior in the UI and OSINT-only curls from sandbox-five-minute.md when you cannot run Neo4j.

docs.title: Redis OOM
docs.text:
• Confirm with redis-cli INFO memory.
  • Reduce ingest pressure (lower producer rate, pause non-critical consumers).
  • Flush non-critical keys only; do not delete core counters without approval.
  • Escalate to platform owner if sustained >15 minutes.

docs.title: Postgres Locks / Deadlocks
docs.text:
• Inspect blockers in pg_stat_activity.
  • Cancel blocking session with highest age and lowest business impact.
  • Enable degraded mode for heavy write paths until lock queue drains.
  • Escalate to DB owner if lock waits persist >10 minutes.

docs.title: Circuit Breaker Storm
docs.text:
• Identify failing dependency from fallback tags and circuit counters.
  • Confirm dependency health before reopening traffic.
  • Temporarily widen backoff and lower concurrent workers.
  • Escalate to service owner if external dependency remains unstable.

docs.title: Escalation Path
docs.text:
• L1: On-call engineer (triage, mitigate, communicate).
  • L2: Service owner (dependency-specific recovery).
  • L3: Platform lead (cross-service rollback/degraded-mode decision).

docs.title: Redis Memory Pressure
docs.text:
• Symptoms: OOM command not allowed, rising latency, dropped writes.
  • Immediate actions:
  • Check memory: redis-cli INFO memory.
  • Enable eviction policy for cache-style keys.
  • Increase memory limit or reduce retention windows for counter/event streams.
  • Follow-up:
  • Lower stream maxlen and aggregate retention.
  • Add alert on used_memory_ratio > 0.85.

docs.title: Postgres Lock Contention
docs.text:
• Symptoms: long transactions, blocked writes, deadlock errors.
  • Immediate actions:
  • Find blockers using pg_stat_activity and pg_locks.
  • Cancel oldest blocking transaction.
  • Reduce concurrent workers in data ingestion until queue stabilizes.
  • Follow-up:
  • Add indexes for frequent filter/order columns.
  • Keep transactions short; avoid large multi-table writes in one txn.

docs.title: Circuit Breaker Storms
docs.text:
• Symptoms: many circuit open errors and fallback reason spikes.
  • Immediate actions:
  • Verify upstream health before raising thresholds.
  • Temporarily increase recovery_seconds to avoid hot-loop retries.
  • Route traffic to degraded mode with explicit analyst banner.
  • Follow-up:
  • Recalibrate per-dependency thresholds from incident metrics.
  • Add SLO alerts on open-circuit counters and fallback ratio.

docs.title: Module Projects Roadmap
docs.text: This section tracks each major module as its own project with a focused roadmap.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
• Each project page contains scope, current gaps, and phased roadmap items.
  • Use the same issue IDs and Friday milestones from the main execution board.
  • Treat Now as active work, Next as near-term queue, and Later as strategic backlog.
  • For cross-module release planning, see Journey ship bundles (J1–J5) alongside the OSS dependency DAG.

docs.title: Modules
docs.text:
• Decision API
  • Case API
  • Graph Service
  • ML Scoring
  • Feature Service
  • Integration Ingress
  • Frontend
  • Python SDK
  • TypeScript SDK
  • Mobile SDKs (Android + iOS)
  • Analytics Sink
  • Investigation Agent — commercial packaging: Saarthi Pro vs OSS

docs.title: Service overview pages (ports & endpoints)
docs.text:
Short write-ups that complement project roadmaps and the API Reference:

  • Decision API · Graph Service · Case API · Feature Service · ML Scoring · Investigation Agent

No dedicated services/*.md page yet for these — use the API Reference: Integration Ingress and Collaboration Chat Bridge ship OpenAPI under contracts/openapi/; Event Ingest and Analytics Sink are documented there as HTTP tables only (no published contracts/openapi spec yet). The GraphQL gateway is code-first Strawberry GraphQL (no contracts/openapi entry).

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Event export, warehouse sync, analytics readiness, and benchmark dataset quality.

docs.title: Current Gaps
docs.text:
• Export quality checks and schema evolution controls need expansion.
  • Analyst-facing benchmark consistency requires stronger governance.

docs.title: Now
docs.text:
• Strengthen export schema validation and backward compatibility checks.
  • Improve benchmark dataset repeatability and seed documentation.

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• Add freshness/SLA telemetry and anomaly reporting for exports.
  • Add release-to-benchmark correlation views.

docs.title: Later
docs.text: • Managed benchmark packs for vertical comparisons and buyer evaluation.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Case lifecycle, dispute operations, queueing, audit trails, and analyst workflow APIs.

docs.title: Current Gaps
docs.text:

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
Workflow assistant capabilities for case triage, evidence gathering, and analyst productivity.

Distribution: The canonical agent implementation is services/investigation-agent in this repo (codename Saarthi). Build a minimal agent image with services/investigation-agent/Dockerfile from the repo root (CI docker-build includes this target). Saarthi-pro is the private commercial product repo (saarthi_pro.asgi, /v1/pro, license middleware, Dockerfile cloning Tarka, registry/RELEASE.md); see its README when you have access. Commercial releases pin a Tarka commit and contract_version (distribution & contract parity); Saarthi-pro does not replace the OSS source of truth for core copilot behavior.

Saarthi Pro (what buyers get): Same copilot behavior and integration contract as the OSS reference; vendor packaging (SLAs where sold, signed DPA/subprocessor story, maintained adapters on supported tiers, release branding). Full comparison → Saarthi Pro vs OSS. Phased commercial plan and artifact index → Saarthi Pro roadmap. Adapter-first economics (illustrative bands; internal until finance/legal sign quotes) → adapter strategy & pricing.

Collaboration chat (Slack, Teams, Lark): optional services/collaboration-chat-bridge forwards platform events to POST /v1/chat, maps thread context into messages[], and surfaces answer_sections (e.g. INFERENCES / NEXT STEPS) in rich messages. Enable COPILOT_STRUCTURED_SECTIONS=true on the agent for reliable section parsing. Deploy stand-alone on AWS/Azure with the bridge + agent containers — see Collaboration chat & cloud.

docs.title: Current gaps (OSS reference)
docs.text:
Area Limitation Grounding & hallucinations Claims trailer + heuristic downgrade of source=tool; token-overlap hints (claims_deterministic_support); optional LLM judge pass; prose is still not formally verified. Truncation and tool errors can still yield confident wrong narratives. Prompt injection & untrusted context Regex/sanitization + optional reject; client platform_audit remains supply-chain into context (mitigated, not removed). Assistant history sanitized as untrusted. Data residency / subprocessors Chat, audit, and tool payloads can reach your configured LLM URL (BYOK). Controls = env flags + network + your DPIA. Auth & blast radius Dev-friendly defaults (ALLOWED_ANALYSTS=*, optional empty API_KEYS); shared upstream_api_key if configured. Hardening knobs exist (COPILOT_REQUIRE_INVESTIGATION_API_KEY, tool denylist). Production profile: COPILOT_PRODUCTION_MODE=true fails startup unless API keys, explicit ALLOWED_ANALYSTS (not *), and OPENAI_API_KEY are set; optional COPILOT_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE, COPILOT_MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES; GET /v1/ready probes data-dir writability; COPILOT_INCLUDE_PLATFORM_AUDIT_IN_PROMPT=true logs a warning in production. See deploy/docker-compose.production-hardening.yml. Powerful tools Replay, label export/ingest, graph, case APIs—safety is upstream API auth + network + RBAC. Product UX & cost SSE POST /v1/chat/stream; sync chat adds turn_id, feedback endpoint, evidence_bundle_draft; offline mode is a single string; token caps env-level. Evidence & benchmarks Shipped: evidence_bundle_draft with v1/dual (schema_id tarka.evidence_bundle/v1), contract_version, tool_trace_redacted, content_sha256; JSON Schema in contracts/schemas/tarka-evidence-bundle-v1.schema.json. Still limited: no adoption of an external industry litigation/evidence-pack standard; no shipped scorecards vs human labels; investigation label drafts ≠ case workflow labels. v1 does not certify factual correctness of prose.

Regional builds: AI_GOVERNANCE_PROFILE overlays and GET /v1/governance are in-repo configuration for policy wording and deployment profiles. See AI governance regional builds.

Intended use, out of scope, and data-flow map (prompts, LLM, RAG, feedback, review DB, logs—plus how regional builds fit): investigation-agent-intended-use-and-data-flows.md.

Integration contract (adapter parity): GET /v1/integration, integration block on GET /v1/health, env INTEGRATION_PROFILE_ID — investigation-agent-integration-contract.md.

Deterministic evidence summary (OSS #40): POST /v1/evidence/summary — no LLM; returns citations with structured resolves_to (trace, case, rule, typology anchors), next_actions from optional typology_breakdown, optional decision_audit anchors on the first citation, and filtered proposed_next_actions (kind=automated_side_effect only when the action id is allow-listed via EVIDENCE_SUMMARY_AUTOMATED_ACTION_ALLOWLIST). Golden fixtures: services/investigation-agent/tests/fixtures/evidence_summary/. OpenAPI: contracts/openapi/investigation-agent.yaml. Human-readable index: API Reference — Investigation Agent.

External CMS / ITSM (ServiceNow, Jira, Salesforce, etc.): The OSS agent calls your Case API (CASE_API_URL); vendor systems integrate via an adapter service (recommended), batch/knowledge sync, or webhooks → chat bridge — catalog and patterns: Investigation CMS and ITSM integrations.

Shipped in tree (keep docs in sync): tool loop to case-api (cases, disputes), graph, decision-api (audit, entity-velocity, replay); paired replay via trace_ids; durable label drafts via /v1/investigation-label-drafts; export of weak labels from cases/disputes; tool-quality metrics + structured logs per chat; batch tabular jobs via POST /v1/batch/ingest (CSV, JSON, NDJSON, XLSX) and tools get_batch_profile, query_batch_rows, aggregate_batch_column (tenant + analyst scoped, in-memory TTL); investigation memos via POST /v1/knowledge/ingest + tool search_knowledge with SQLite-backed chunks under INVESTIGATION_DATA_DIR (optional hybrid RAG: embeddings when OPENAI_API_KEY is set and copilot_knowledge_embeddings is true; copilot_rag_keyword_weight, copilot_embedding_model, COPILOT_RAG_DB_NAME); deterministic queue snapshot tool compare_entity_queue_snapshot (entity velocity + list_cases window overlap); structured answer sections (Markdown headings FACTS / INFERENCES / UNKNOWNS / NEXT STEPS, toggle COPILOT_STRUCTURED_SECTIONS); claims_deterministic_support + tool_acknowledgment_warnings on each chat; optional judge pass (COPILOT_ENABLE_JUDGE_PASS, OPENAI_JUDGE_MODEL); evidence_bundle_draft on each turn (v0 / v1 / dual, see COPILOT_EVIDENCE_BUNDLE_FORMAT); POST /v1/feedback with SQLite persistence (COPILOT_FEEDBACK_DB_NAME), optional tenant_id/analyst_id on the body, GET /v1/feedback/summary and GET /v1/feedback/recent for analytics; optional analytics events (COPILOT_ANALYTICS_*: turn completed, feedback submitted); strict assurance mode (COPILOT_ASSURANCE_MODE=strict) withholds model prose when tool errors are unacknowledged or tool-claims fail deterministic support; optional derived_facts (server-extracted tool scalars, COPILOT_DERIVED_FACTS or strict); POST /v1/review/turn and GET /v1/review/turn for human sign-off records (COPILOT_REVIEW_DB_NAME); POST /v1/chat/stream (SSE); maker–checker for sensitive tools when COPILOT_REVIEWER_SECRET is set (hides ingest_labeled_rows / run_replay_ab_comparison unless x-reviewer-secret matches); typology playbooks via GET /v1/playbooks and optional playbook_id on chat; source reference cards (source_refs); regional AI governance builds (AI_GOVERNANCE_PROFILE=us|eu_uk|global) with GET /v1/governance and compose/Helm profiles under deploy/profiles/ai-governance/ (see AI governance regional builds). Optional production hardening overlay: deploy/docker-compose.production-hardening.yml. Prompt contract version in GET /v1/health (copilot_prompt_version, copilot_features includes knowledge_embeddings, feedback_persistence, assurance_mode, derived_facts, turn_review_persistence, evidence_bundle_format, evidence_bundle_v1, analytics_*).

Assurance (optional): COPILOT_ASSURANCE_MODE=standard|strict (strict refusal when tool errors are unacknowledged in prose or tool-sourced claims lack deterministic overlap with tool JSON); COPILOT_DERIVED_FACTS (include server-extracted derived_facts on chat; implied in strict); human sign-off POST /v1/review/turn + GET /v1/review/turn (SQLite COPILOT_REVIEW_DB_NAME). Guide: investigation-agent-assurance-modes.md.

Copilot hardening (OSS env): COPILOT_INJECTION_POLICY (sanitize | reject), COPILOT_INCLUDE_PLATFORM_AUDIT_IN_PROMPT, COPILOT_REQUIRE_INVESTIGATION_API_KEY, COPILOT_MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS, COPILOT_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS, COPILOT_ENFORCE_TOOL_CLAIM_GROUNDING, COPILOT_DISABLED_TOOLS, COPILOT_HIDE_TOOLS_WITHOUT_UPSTREAM (default true — hide tools whose upstream URL is unset), COPILOT_STRUCTURED_SECTIONS, COPILOT_ENABLE_JUDGE_PASS, OPENAI_JUDGE_MODEL, COPILOT_JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS, COPILOT_REVIEWER_SECRET, COPILOT_SENSITIVE_TOOLS, KNOWLEDGE_TTL_SECONDS, COPILOT_PROMPT_VERSION, COPILOT_ASSURANCE_MODE, COPILOT_DERIVED_FACTS, COPILOT_REVIEW_DB_NAME, COPILOT_EVIDENCE_BUNDLE_FORMAT (v0 | v1 | dual, default dual), AGENT_BUILD_ID, COPILOT_EVIDENCE_REDACTION_LEVEL (none | analyst_view | export_safe), COPILOT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED, COPILOT_ANALYTICS_SINK (log | http), COPILOT_ANALYTICS_WEBHOOK_URL, COPILOT_ANALYTICS_HMAC_SECRET. Integration changelog: CHANGELOG_INTEGRATION. Details: investigation-agent-llm-data-flow.md.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: Saarthi Pro (commercial packaging, procurement, certification): see Saarthi Pro roadmap. The bullets below are OSS reference priorities in this repo.

docs.title: Now
docs.text:
• Workflows & reports (OSS): JSON workflow manifests under GET /v1/workflows; workflow_id + workflow_params on POST /v1/chat append SOP-style system instructions (first shipped: sop_case_summary_v1 for case-summary narrative). POST /v1/reports/case-summary returns a case-summary PDF from client-supplied reply + answer_sections + claims (no server-side replay). POST /v1/reports/turn-bundle returns Markdown + structured JSON for the same paste-from-chat handoff. Extend with more manifests and trend/report jobs as data paths land.
  • Reference / minimal-integration profile (OSS): GET /v1/setup — checklist (LLM key, embedding/RAG, optional upstream URLs) plus integration snapshot. Env: COPILOT_REFERENCE_MODE, COPILOT_PLAIN_CHAT, COPILOT_PLAIN_PREFETCH_RAG, COPILOT_EMBEDDING_BASE_URL / COPILOT_EMBEDDING_API_KEY for a separate OpenAI-compatible embeddings server. POST /v1/chat returns turn_metrics (model, llm_completion_rounds, tool_surface, usage). Example env: services/investigation-agent/.env.reference.example. Batch-ingest script for wiki Markdown dirs: services/investigation-agent/scripts/ingest_wiki_markdown.py. Golden prompts pack for regression: investigation_agent/resources/golden_prompts_v1.json.
  • Production guardrails (OSS): COPILOT_PRODUCTION_MODE, rate/body limits, GET /v1/ready, production block on GET /v1/health — see deploy/docker-compose.production-hardening.yml and investigation_agent/production_config.py. Does not replace TLS, network policy, or upstream RBAC.
  • Extend evidence_bundle toward external evidence-pack or review workflows when a sector schema lands; tighten deterministic traces and scorecard hooks for analyst trust (see evidence bundle v1 alignment spec—v1 fields already shipped in OSS).
  • Add guardrails for action suggestions and policy-safe defaults.

docs.title: Release v1.0.2 (patch)
docs.text: Patch release: CI/security ergonomics and manual workflow reruns.

docs.title: Changes
docs.text:
• CodeQL Advanced and Security scan workflows declare workflow_dispatch: {} so GitHub registers manual runs: use Run workflow in the Actions tab or gh workflow run "CodeQL Advanced" --ref master / gh workflow run "Security scan" --ref master (ref must match your default branch).
  • CodeQL (Swift / iOS SPM): build order and set -e-safe helpers; CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS=true to keep PR file-coverage behavior when GitHub defaults change (April 2026+ notice).
  • Security scan: Trivy SARIF uploads use github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v4 (already on trunk before this tag where applicable).
  • Docker images: base apt-get upgrade / apk upgrade steps for security fixes (see recent ci(security) commits).
  • Benchmark smoke: drift_score_smoke.py job for heuristic drift separation (see scripts/benchmarks/).

docs.title: Version bumps
docs.text:
• Root Python meta package tarka 1.0.0 → 1.0.2 (pyproject.toml).
  • Frontend tarka-ui 1.0.2 (frontend/package.json + lockfile root).
  • tarka CLI installer state default version string aligned to 1.0.2.

Service-level pyproject.toml versions are unchanged unless they already tracked 1.0.x independently.

docs.title: GitHub issues
docs.text: No open bug-labeled issues in the repo mapped 1:1 to this patch; roadmap enhancement items remain open by design.

docs.title: Troubleshooting (CodeQL rerun)
docs.text:
If gh workflow run codeql.yml returns 422 (“disabled workflow”): the workflow file was turned off in the Actions UI. Re-enable it:

gh workflow enable codeql.yml

If gh workflow run "CodeQL Advanced" targets the wrong workflow ID (duplicate names): prefer gh workflow run codeql.yml --ref master.

Stale workflows pointing at removed files (e.g. a former advancedcodeql.yml) can be disabled in Actions → … → Disable workflow to avoid duplicate CodeQL runs alongside dynamic/github-code-scanning/codeql (default setup).

docs.title: Release v1.1.0 (Planned)
docs.text: Planned release date: 2026-04-30

docs.title: Theme
docs.text:
Consortium intelligence v2, inference contract v2, analyst-facing explainability, plus CI/security hygiene and a documented five-minute eval path—one coordinated ship.

v1.1.0 is still treated as an early-development beta train: release evidence is mandatory, and any temporary waiver must be explicit, scoped, time-bound, and tracked to closure.

docs.title: Required documentation pack (v1.1.0 gate)
docs.text:
These documents are required to treat v1.1.0 as release-ready. Keep this table current as the final RC SHA is validated.

Required doc Purpose Status Source SECURITY.md Responsible disclosure and security contact path Present [SECURITY.md](../../../SECURITY.md) LICENSE-DEPENDENCIES.md Third-party/license posture for shipped stack Present [LICENSE-DEPENDENCIES.md](../../../LICENSE-DEPENDENCIES.md) CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md Community contribution and behavior policy Present [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../../../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) Security scanning guide Operator-facing Trivy/scan workflow guidance Present [docs/docs/guides/security-scanning.md](../guides/security-scanning.md) Five-minute sandbox runbook Repeatable smoke path for evaluate + OSINT + UI Present [docs/docs/guides/sandbox-five-minute.md](../guides/sandbox-five-minute.md) Release schedule alignment Planned trains and scope guardrails Present [RELEASE_SCHEDULE.md](../../../RELEASE_SCHEDULE.md) Versioned release note Canonical acceptance checklist and evidence log Present [docs/docs/releases/v1.1.0-2026-04-30.md](v1.1.0-2026-04-30.md)

docs.title: Pending tasks before tagging v1.1.0
docs.text:
• Run full CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) on the exact release candidate SHA in a main/master-scoped workflow context and record the run URL in this file. Evidence: master SHA df3224c3b3e5056d03554ca9732fde2f25086139 — CI run 24377895049.
  • Confirm benchmark-latency-evaluate artifact exists for the same RC SHA and replace the “workflow-scope gated” placeholder with the artifact/run URL. Evidence: local artifact from RC smoke at [benchmark-latency-evaluate-local.txt](../../../benchmark-latency-evaluate-local.txt) (requests=20, concurrency=1, all 200).
  • Run security-scan.yml (Trivy) on the same RC SHA and replace pending/gated notes with the final run URL. Evidence: master SHA df3224c3b3e5056d03554ca9732fde2f25086139 — Security scan run 24377895059.
  • Run secret-scan.yml (TruffleHog) on the same RC SHA and replace pending/gated notes with the final run URL. Evidence / waiver: beta-only temporary waiver — GitHub Actions API returns no runs for secret-scan.yml on master as of 2026-04-19. Scope: v1.1.0 tag readiness only. Expiry: first post-tag week or first successful main/master run, whichever comes first. Required follow-up: attach run URL and remove waiver text.
  • Re-verify bash scripts/ci/verify_release_candidate.sh against the intended tag commit and refresh evidence date/SHA if changed. Evidence: local run on release/v1.2.0 SHA ec89ac96e47d47b56e9df0cf6e7e382d4bbea6ee completed successfully (ruff, frontend build, investigation-agent tests, TS SDK build).
  • Re-check no open P0 blockers on demo path (8000 evaluate, 8003 OSINT, 3000 UI) and note any explicit waiver in checklist evidence before tag. Evidence: GitHub issue search is:open label:P0 for pamu512/tarka returned 0; demo smoke confirms 3000 HTTP 200, 8000 evaluate success, 8003 OSINT success.

docs.title: Recent commits folded into this release context
docs.text:
Recent changes on trunk/release lines and why they matter for Tarka as an overall stack:

  • 860aeb2 (feat(epic-c)): adds distinct_session_id_24h to velocity manifest, improving feature parity between counters, replay, and decisioning.
  • 5d9f435 (fix(ci)): resolves decision-api lint/gate failures, improving build stability and release confidence.
  • 573b2c1 (fix(location)): tightens IP-geo gating and signal parsing, improving signal quality and reducing enrichment noise.
  • 84d4a8d and 3499264 (feat(location)): adds calibration status and geo/co-presence plumbing across SDK/API/graph, improving explainability and operational monitoring.
  • 6cfcf74 (chore(ci)): adds security/benchmark gate prep, improving release evidence quality for this beta train.
  • ed6dc64 (feat): expands investigation copilot and collaboration bridge readiness, improving analyst response workflows.

docs.title: Forward expectations (v1.2.0, v1.3.0)
docs.text:
• v1.2.0 (2026-05-30): productized vertical packs, benchmark scorecards, counter/velocity parity hardening, and operator reliability scorecards.
  • v1.3.0 (2026-06-29): Trust Center, evidence exports, signed release artifacts, and enterprise governance hardening.

v1.1.0 remains the early-development beta foundation for these trains: stabilize the core decision path first, then layer productization (v1.2.0) and governance depth (v1.3.0).

docs.title: Consortium (quality and policy)
docs.text:
In production code today (HTTP + Redis + unit tests): weighted consortium scoring with per-tenant trust; **POST /v1/consortium/feedback outcomes (**false_positive / confirmed_fraud) feed FP-rate and quality-style fields used when applying **consortium_score_delta**; **POST /v1/consortium/share**, **GET /v1/consortium/check/...**, **POST /v1/consortium/trust**; gating via **CONSORTIUM_*** env and **consortium_min_tenants** (see services/decision-api/src/decision_api/consortium_api.py, redis_store.py, consortium.py, tests/test_consortium.py).

Product surface: there is no consortium-specific Case / React dashboard in v1.1; “tenant participation” is configuration + API, not a separate analyst policy console. Aggregate decision throughput is illustrated by **services/decision-api/static/dashboard.html (general events, not consortium KPIs). A fuller Trust Center / consortium posture UI is roadmapped for later trains** (see [v1.2.0-2026-05-30.md](v1.2.0-2026-05-30.md), [v1.3.0-2026-06-29.md](v1.3.0-2026-06-29.md)).

docs.title: Inference and decision contract (Epics A, B, E—core)
docs.text:
• Versioned **inference_context (schema v2) with **confidence_tier (low / medium / high).
  • **driver_reasons**: analyst-oriented top drivers (tamper, replay, network, geo, ML, rules, colocation, travel proxy).
  • Replay / tamper context reflected in derived risks and drivers (ingress replay signals, rule-based replay hints).
  • Co-location and impossible-travel proxies (colocation_risk, impossible_travel_risk) from device and velocity/geo heuristics.
  • Velocity in inference: velocity_events_5m, velocity_events_1h, velocity_events_24h (aggregates include 5m window).
  • **recommended_action** on evaluate and audit responses (e.g. block, manual_review, step_up_mfa, step_up_attestation) for client step-up hints.

docs.title: CI/CD, security hygiene, and first-run polish (ships with v1.1.0 train)
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions CI (main / master): Ruff; decision-api tests with coverage gate (≥48% as enforced in **.github/workflows/ci.yml, path to 60%+); case-api, Python SDK; graph-service (Neo4j-mocked algorithms); integration-ingress (full suite + OSINT parallel tests, PYTHONPATH includes **services/shared); investigation-agent; graphql-gateway, event-ingest, analytics-sink, feature-service, ml-scoring (pytest + coverage XML where configured); frontend npm ci + **npm run build; **packages/fraud-sdk-typescript **npm ci + npm run build**; **benchmark-latency-evaluate** (lite stack + **scripts/benchmarks/latency_evaluate.py** artifact); Docker builds gated on all test and Node build jobs.
  • PostgreSQL schema: Alembic migrations under **services/decision-api/alembic/ and **services/case-api/alembic/ — **alembic upgrade head** on service startup when using PostgreSQL; SQLite (CI/unit) still uses **create_all**.
  • GraphQL gateway exposes the same **/metrics and structured request logging as other FastAPI services (**services/shared/observability.py).
  • Security scanning workflow: Trivy filesystem + decision-api image → SARIF upload (where code scanning is enabled); weekly schedule.
  • Dependabot: grouped updates for GitHub Actions, pip (core services), npm (frontend).
  • Docs: **SECURITY.md (responsible disclosure), **LICENSE-DEPENDENCIES.md (Neo4j AGPL / lite and alternates), **CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md**, **docs/docs/guides/security-scanning.md**, **docs/docs/guides/sandbox-five-minute.md** (copy-paste evaluate + OSINT + UI path).
  • Onboarding: **.devcontainer/devcontainer.json (Codespaces / Docker-outside-Docker); README badges (CI, security scan, Codespaces); maintainer Loom walkthrough on [Tarka root README.md](../../../README.md)** — five-minute sandbox + Case Detail ([pamu512/tarka](https://github.com/pamu512/tarka)), not other repositories.
  • **deploy/docker-compose.lite.yml: adds integration-ingress (8003**) so lite stack matches the five-minute OSINT demo without full Neo4j.

Documented stretch (also on trunk): GraphQL **evaluate returns **recommended_action and **inference_context (JSON) from the decision-api payload — services/graphql-gateway/src/graphql_gateway/schema.py, services/graphql-gateway/tests/test_gateway.py. The [Tarka repository root README.md](../../../README.md) (canonical GitHub view) links the maintainer Loom walkthrough (five-minute sandbox + Case Detail) under the v1.1 train bullets — this is the fraud-stack (tarka) README, not Skuld or other sibling repos.**

Gap-closure schedule (what ships 4/30 vs 5/30): [docs/docs/guides/release-gap-closure-schedule.md](../guides/release-gap-closure-schedule.md).

docs.title: Release candidate finalization & May handoff policy
docs.text:
Goal for v1.1.0: ship with no failing automated release gates and no known P0 blockers on the demo path (lite compose + five-minute doc) and the full stack profile used in CI (docker-build / stack-smoke matrix). “Zero bugs” is not mathematically provable; operational definition = all required CI jobs pass, security workflows complete without blocking failures, and open P0 issues affecting demo or tagged stack are closed or explicitly waived in the checklist above.

Minimum bar before tagging v1.1.0:

  1. **[ci.yml](../../../.github/workflows/ci.yml) — full matrix green on the release candidate commit: lint (Ruff); frontend npm ci + npm run build; **investigation-agent tests; decision-api (coverage gate), case-api, graph-service, Neo4j live job, integration-ingress, SDK Python, GraphQL, event-ingest, analytics-sink, feature-service, ml-scoring; TypeScript SDK build; docker-build; stack-smoke as configured.
  2. **[security-scan.yml](../../../.github/workflows/security-scan.yml) and **[secret-scan.yml](../../../.github/workflows/secret-scan.yml) — completed runs on the same commit (Trivy + secret scanning findings gate), or for early-development beta only an explicit, time-bound waiver documented in this checklist with owner and closure date.
  3. Manual smoke: [sandbox-five-minute.md](../guides/sandbox-five-minute.md) (evaluate + OSINT + Case detail explainability + copilot/mock path as applicable).

After v1.1.0 is tagged (finalized build): routine product and engineering changes that miss the 4/30 tag do not retroactively alter v1.1.0. They ship on the May 2026 Friday trains documented in [release-calendar-2026-05.md](../guides/release-calendar-2026-05.md) and roll into **v1.2.0 (2026-05-30) per [RELEASE_SCHEDULE.md](../../../RELEASE_SCHEDULE.md), unless explicitly cherry-picked as a patch** (document any exception in release notes).

Local subset (frontend + copilot + TS SDK + Ruff on services/investigation-agent): from repo root, bash scripts/ci/verify_release_candidate.sh — does not replace **ruff check . on the whole repo, decision-api coverage, Docker build, stack-smoke, or other service pytests (those stay on GitHub Actions ci.yml**).

docs.title: Maintainer runbook (push → PR → CI → RC script → evidence → tag)
docs.text:
1. Commit on a branch (e.g. release/v1.1.0-rc or chore/v1.1.0-prep) with all intended changes; do not commit local .coverage or services/**/*.db (see root .gitignore).
  2. Push the branch to origin and open a pull request to main / master (GitHub UI or gh pr create). Sole maintainer: PR is still useful so CI runs on the branch; self-merge after green is fine.
  3. Wait until **[ci.yml](../../../.github/workflows/ci.yml) and **[security-scan.yml](../../../.github/workflows/security-scan.yml) complete successfully on the PR (or merge commit).
  4. On Linux or macOS, from repo root: bash scripts/ci/verify_release_candidate.sh On Windows, fix npm ci issues (file locks / AV) or run the same Node steps in WSL; the script’s Ruff slice targets services/investigation-agent only—full ruff check . is still the lint job in CI.
  5. Fill evidence in the acceptance checklist tables above (links to Actions runs, PR, screenshots).
  6. Merge the PR when green. On the exact merge commit (or chosen RC SHA), tag: git tag -a v1.1.0 -m "v1.1.0 — see docs/docs/releases/v1.1.0-2026-04-30.md" git push origin v1.1.0 Do not tag until steps 3–5 are satisfied.

docs.title: Single release sign-off (v1.1.0)
docs.text:
One “go” for 2026-04-30 after the tables above are complete (or explicitly waived with evidence). With a sole maintainer, engineering / product scope / routine security hygiene roll into one attestation—no separate signatures required unless a third party (e.g. auditor) demands it later.

Role Name Signature / date Notes Maintainer (engineering, scope, release hygiene) Anoop Pamu 04/22/2026 Self-attest: CI 24322755744, Security 24322755732, lite smoke 2026-04-13 (see Pre-tag validation table).

docs.title: Planned Breaking Changes
docs.text: • None expected. Clients should tolerate new optional/v2 fields; older clients ignoring unknown JSON keys remain compatible.

docs.text:
• Integration tests for weighted consortium scoring.
  • End-to-end feedback loop tests from case outcome to decision score impact.
  • API and UI tests for consortium policy controls.
  • Contract tests for evaluate/audit **inference_context v2 and **recommended_action.
  • **pytest (decision-api) and frontend **npm run build** green before tag; TypeScript SDK **npm run build green.
  • CI workflow green on default branch: lint, all Python service test jobs (including graphql + streaming path services), Node builds, Docker build matrix.
  • Trivy security workflow completes (SARIF upload may depend on org plan); Dependabot enabled for the repository.

docs.title: Release v1.2.0 (Planned)
docs.text:
Planned release date: 2026-05-30

Ship hold: v1.2.0 is not finalized (no publishable tag/release) until Epic C completion (10x bar) is met. See RELEASE_SCHEDULE.md § v1.2.0 — ship hold until Epic C “10x”.

docs.title: Theme
docs.text: Vertical intelligence starter packs and benchmarkability—with explicit concentration on closing competitive gaps called out in competitive-critical-review-2026-04.md.

docs.title: Strategic concentration (5/30 train)
docs.text:
Engineering and product work for v1.2.0 prioritizes these seven pillars (maps to Day 60 scope in roadmap-30-60-90.md and release-gap-closure-schedule.md):

Pillar Intent for 5/30 Productized quality Opinionated vertical packs, deterministic benchmarks, preset governance profiles—fewer raw knobs, clearer outcomes. Device / session / integrity Stronger packaged tamper/replay/MitM and session signal story across SDKs and inference; consistency controls. Location & co-presence Richer location/co-presence signals and rule-facing keys; documented confidence semantics where not covered by inference proxies alone. Trunk already has: optional browser GPS + opt-in IP geo (ENABLE_IP_GEO_LOOKUP), location_context merge + geo mismatch tags, graph Place / SEEN_AT — v1.2 acceptance should cite tests + operator docs, not re-implement from scratch. Counters / velocity as a platform feature-service multi-window counters, normalized velocity keys, online/offline parity per counter-replay-parity.md. Analyst workflows Templates, reason-code mappings, tighter decision→review loops; extends v1.1.0 explainability. Network / consortium effects Deepen consortium-weighted intelligence and tenant policy surfaces where they improve collective signal without sacrificing tenant isolation. Operations & lock-in inverse Connector reliability scorecards, ingress SLA-style fields, documented deploy profiles and graph-backend alternates—reduce operational and legal friction vs closed vendors.

Deliverables below (starter packs, benchmarks, counters, ingress reliability) roll up into these pillars; issue ordering still follows oss-ship-order-dependencies.md.

docs.title: Graph service: Neo4j ↔ JanusGraph (5/30)
docs.text:
Ship: services/graph-service can run against Neo4j (default) or JanusGraph via Gremlin Server by setting GRAPH_BACKEND and JANUSGRAPH_* env vars. The HTTP API is unchanged—Decision API, case flows, and investigation / copilot clients keep the same GRAPH_SERVICE_URL contract.

  • Operator guide: services/graph-service/docs/janusgraph-adapter.md.
  • Analytics: Janus mode uses Gremlin + in-process algorithms; fraud-ring detection is documented as an approximation vs Neo4j Cypher (same response shape, not guaranteed identical rings).

docs.title: Journey coverage (J1–J5)
docs.text:
How this release advances user journeys (see that guide for full issue/release mapping):

Journey Role Planned emphasis in v1.2.0 J1 — Integrate Integrations / platform Deeper connector reliability and ingress-facing surfaces (aligned with integration health and probe-style controls). J2 — Decide Risk / automation Primary: vertical starter packs, benchmark harness, preset profiles for model/rule governance—typology- and promotion-aligned shipping. J3 — Investigate Analyst Workflow templates and reason-code mappings that tie decisions to review behavior; explainability surfaces as capacity allows. J4 — Govern Compliance / leadership Light prep only where packs and benchmarks produce auditable artifacts; heavy J4 is v1.3.0. J5 — Operate SRE / platform Deterministic benchmarks and regression passes as operational guardrails; scorecard-oriented outcomes where shipped.

docs.title: DAO integration (scope for 5/30)
docs.text:
DAO here means decentralized community or consortium governance that should influence risk signal sharing, not the in-app “governance” of model promotion (that stays YAML/CI-first in v1.2).

Layer Fit with Tarka today v1.2.0 (5/30) Later (e.g. v1.3+ / J4) Off-chain DAO → operational policy Decisions already support preset profiles and consortium hooks (consortium_api, Redis fraud:consortium:*). Document how a DAO’s off-chain votes map to env/config (CONSORTIUM_*, trust scores, opt-in participation). Optional OpenAPI + guide for consortium HTTP APIs. UI / Trust Center surfacing of consortium posture. On-chain attestations → consortium signals record_consortium_signal / add_consortium_feedback can carry provenance if callers attach signed metadata. Spike or thin adapter: e.g. a small ingest worker (or documented curl recipe) that turns signed DAO payloads (IPFS snapshot, EIP-191 message, or org-specific format) into consortium API calls—no chain dependency in core services. Hardened identity, replay protection, and audit exports (aligns with v1.3 evidence story). Token voting inside the product No wallet or chain coupling in core stack today. Out of scope for v1.2 unless explicitly prioritized; treat as separate product track. Revisit if J4 requires on-chain evidence bundles.

Dependencies: stays aligned with the Network / consortium effects pillar and competitive-critical-review-2026-04.md “collective signal without sacrificing tenant isolation.” Tenant isolation remains non-negotiable: DAO-fed signals are opt-in per deployment and must not imply cross-tenant PII leakage.

Exit criteria for v1.2 DAO slice (pick one or two):

  1. Published integration guide under docs/docs/guides/ describing how DAO outcomes map to consortium APIs (sequence: DAO outcome → consortium endpoints → evaluate behavior) — dao-to-consortium-integration.md.
  2. Reference adapter under scripts/consortium_adapter/: CLI + JSON Lines ingest + Python client (httpx).
  3. If a chain attestation format is chosen, document one canonical example (versioned) in contracts/ or docs/docs/guides/examples/ — contracts/examples/dao-attestation-v1.example.json.

Reference (factual pattern only, no vendor endorsement): joinsonar-query-feedback-vs-consortium-api.md and unit21-fraud-dao-vs-consortium-api.md compare public consortium-style flows to Tarka’s consortium_api.

Heavy J4 (Trust Center, signed release governance) remains v1.3.0 per v1.3.0-2026-06-29.md.

docs.title: Planned Highlights
docs.text:
• Fintech, e-commerce, and gaming starter packs.
  • Vertical workflow templates and reason-code mappings.
  • Benchmark harness for baseline vs vertical-pack lift.
  • Preset profile selection in model/rule governance workflows.
  • Publishable numbers: document standard hardware + compose profile for scripts/benchmarks/latency_evaluate.py and simulation KPIs; extend with optional k6/hey recipes if needed.
  • Examples index maintained under docs/docs/guides/examples/ aligned with shipped vertical packs.
  • Counter replay / online-offline parity implementation per counter-replay-parity.md (worker + CI golden counters).
  • Load recipes: optional hey script scripts/benchmarks/load-hey-evaluate.sh for publishable RPS/latency; extend to k6 if needed.

docs.title: ML explainability (shipping on the 5/30 train)
docs.text:
Shipped (trunk target for v1.2.0):

  • ml-scoring POST /v1/score returns structured ml_top_factors (ranked codes + descriptions + impact) and a single-line ml_summary, derived from existing heuristic / ONNX / adaptive explanations (FraudLens-style factor list + narrative, without a runtime dependency on external demos).
  • decision-api passes that slice through _fetch_ml_score into inference_context: optional ml_top_factors, ml_summary, ml_model (plus driver_reasons entries ml_factor:<CODE> for the top factors).
  • Contracts: OpenAPI InferenceContext in contracts/openapi/decision-api.yaml; golden example contracts/golden/inference-context-v2.example.json; Python + TypeScript SDK types; Case Detail ML explanation block in the frontend.

Stretch (implemented; optional at runtime, behind extras / env):

  • TreeSHAP + LightGBM: pip install -e "services/ml-scoring[shap]" (or tarka-ml-scoring[shap] from the package), then set ML_SHAP_ENABLED=1 and ML_LGBM_MODEL_PATH to a joblib-pickled LGBMClassifier / LGBMRegressor using the same 9-feature order as services/ml-scoring/src/ml_scoring/main.py. When active, POST /v1/score uses the tree model score for the adaptive blend base and ml_top_factors from TreeSHAP. Health JSON includes shap_stretch_enabled.
  • Batch scoring CLI: scripts/ml_batch_score.py (CSV → POST /v1/score); documented in scripts/benchmarks/README.md.
  • DAO → consortium: integration guide docs/docs/guides/dao-to-consortium-integration.md; attestation example contracts/examples/dao-attestation-v1.example.json.

docs.title: Documentation vs code (rolling tracker)
docs.text:
Use this table to close gaps between marketing language and the repo. Update it as work merges to master.

Topic Status Notes v1.1 consortium bullets Doc aligned v1.1.0-2026-04-30.md § Consortium now distinguishes API/Redis vs Case UI (no consortium dashboard in React for v1.1). DAO → consortium guide Living dao-to-consortium-integration.md — HTTP paths must match POST /v1/consortium/share (not /signal). Graph: Neo4j vs JanusGraph Shipped GRAPH_BACKEND / Gremlin path documented in services/graph-service/docs/janusgraph-adapter.md. Counter replay / golden counters Partial Manifest v1 + GET /v1/internal/counters/manifest; POST /v1/internal/counters/replay / POST /v1/internal/counters/replay/from-audit (token: COUNTER_REPLAY_TOKEN / X-Tarka-Counter-Replay-Token); OpenAPI paths in contracts/openapi/decision-api.yaml; scripts replay_aggregates.py, export_audit_to_jsonl.py, diff_aggregate_redis.py; operator playbook redis-agg-key-version-migration.md; CI test_golden_counters.py, counter-parity-smoke.yml. Remaining: production cutover evidence per playbook. ML top factors / summary in inference_context Shipped on trunk decision_api + ml-scoring + Case Detail UI (CaseDetail.tsx); keep golden keys + integration tests green per § Planned Validation below. Vertical packs + benchmark harness Partial decision-api exposes and documents GET /v1/rules/vertical-packs, POST /v1/rules/vertical-packs/{vertical_name}/install, and POST /v1/simulation/benchmark/vertical; tests cover list/install/benchmark flows; benchmark smoke adds vertical_benchmark_smoke.py and CI job in .github/workflows/benchmark-smoke.yml. Remaining: publishable scorecards + acceptance thresholds. Feature-service multi-window counters Partial Shared module fraud_aggregates.py; normalized_velocity_key_names() includes distinct_session_id_24h when session_id is present in the payload (same pattern as device_id); feature-service reads velocity_counters on POST /v1/snapshot and POST /v1/velocity/query when REDIS_URL or FEATURE_SERVICE_REDIS_URL is set (same Redis / fraud:agg:* keys as decision-api). Remaining: richer rule-pack docs + optional compose env wiring. Challenge policy templates Partial JSON in rules/challenge_policies/ (default_v1, strict_review_v1, payments_high_value_v1); optional challenge_policy_id on evaluate; response challenge_metadata + GET /v1/challenge-policies; env CHALLENGE_POLICY_DEFAULT. Ingress reliability scorecards Partial API-first reliability surfaces documented in contracts/openapi/integration-ingress.yaml (/v1/slo, readiness, health-matrix, connectivity, vault endpoints) plus new GET /v1/integrations/scorecards; scorecards derived from installed-provider connectivity snapshots (last_connectivity_test) with normalized status (healthy/degraded/down/unknown) and overall score. Remaining: historical trend windows + UI panel polish. Location SDK + graph coherence (GPS / IP / Place) Shipped on trunk packages/fraud-sdk-typescript (enableGeo), packages/fraud-sdk-python (enable_ip_geo), decision_api/location_context.py, graph Place/SEEN_AT, OpenAPI DeviceContext.signals — keep .github/workflows/ci.yml + decision-api tests green; document env flags in SDK guides.

docs.title: Suggested build order (engineering)
docs.text:
1. Lock parity docs: keep DAO/consortium paths and examples aligned with consortium_api.py (this pass fixed /share).
  2. Counter platform (Epic C): manifest v1 + token-gated POST /v1/internal/counters/replay + GET .../manifest shipped; next: Redis key-prefix versioning, audit-export batch job — see counter-replay-parity.md.
  3. Feature-service: shared fraud_aggregates + snapshot / /v1/velocity/query wired — extend rule-pack examples and compose defaults (see Day 60 in roadmap-30-60-90.md).
  4. Policy templates (Epic D): challenge orchestration configs in decision-api; frontend panels when APIs exist.
  5. Vertical packs + benchmarks: wire scripts/benchmarks/ to reproducible scorecards; maintain docs/docs/guides/examples/.
  6. Ingress: reliability fields and scorecards on integration-ingress as APIs stabilize.

docs.title: Release v1.3.0 (Planned)

docs.title: Journey coverage (J1–J5)
docs.text:
How this release advances user journeys (see that guide for full issue/release mapping):

Journey Role Planned emphasis in v1.3.0 J1 — Integrate Integrations / platform Indirect: Trust Center and evidence exports should reference deployment profiles and integration posture where controls apply. J2 — Decide Risk / automation Evidence exports include decision/policy lineage suitable for audit; promotion and typology version metadata where contracts allow. J3 — Investigate Analyst Evidence export APIs and bundle-shaped outputs support investigation narratives and procurement evidence (feeds #50 / agent alignment). J4 — Govern Compliance / leadership Primary: Trust Center UI, evidence export APIs, CI release-governance checklist, signed artifacts—procurement-ready posture. J5 — Operate SRE / platform Release gates, signed builds, and operational readiness views in Trust Center; runbook and health linkage as acceptance criteria.
